spec_match.rationale: No-developer-visible-change feature — by definition the API doesn't expose the new behaviour, so the demo can only probe indirectly. Honest about the limit but doesn't fully match the spec's framing.

interactive.rationale: 3 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects / 0 ranges driving live JS — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Either the feature-index blurb or the concept lede is missing — can't verify alignment.

edge_cases.rationale: Demo runs the happy path; no visible feature detection, error handling, or no-support fallback in the script.

references.rationale: No chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link on the concept page — CLAUDE.md rule #3 says every page must carry the self-heal reference, not rely on the parent feature index.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from the shared CSS variables and the layout follows the stage + controls editorial pattern.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: screen-reader output before vs after
openQuestions.detail: The don-t-use-aria-details-for-anchor-positioning feature's headline framing — Anchor-positioned elements no longer auto-emit aria-details (popovers still do) — calls out "screen-reader output before vs after". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: developer-supplied aria-describedby / aria-details as the ex
openQuestions.detail: The don-t-use-aria-details-for-anchor-positioning feature's headline framing — Anchor-positioned elements no longer auto-emit aria-details (popovers still do) — calls out "developer-supplied aria-describedby / aria-details as the explicit replacement". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature link to this page
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, every concept page must include the https://chromestatus.com/feature/5068815373303808 link directly — it is the self-heal anchor used by the slug-recovery script. Right now the page links only back to the parent feature index.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one realistic failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Add feature detection at the top of the script and surface the result. Handle the permission-denied / API-rejection case in the catch branch with a visible message. If the feature is flag-gated or origin-trial, show explicitly what the page does when the API is absent.

openQuestions.title: Align the concept lede with the feature-index blurb
openQuestions.detail: The blurb on the feature index sells one promise; the concept lede frames a different angle. Either reword the lede to echo the blurb's keywords (so visitors recognise they're in the right place) or update the parent blurb.

summary: screen-reader-trace under don-t-use-aria-details-for-anchor-positioning: 2 pass / 3 partial / 1 fail. Strong interactive surface, design system clean, spec story is partial — see open questions for the angles still missing.
